message:
docs: add Mermaid section, troubleshooting FAQ, and comparison examples

- Getting Started: add fromMermaid quick-start section and
  troubleshooting FAQ for common issues
- Comparison: add side-by-side code example showing drawmode vs
  raw Excalidraw JSON vs Mermaid for the same diagram

files.patch:
@@ -20,6 +20,45 @@ Several MCP-based tools can generate diagrams from AI coding assistants. This pa
 | Sequence diagrams | Yes (`addActor`/`message`) | Manual | Yes | Yes |
 | Code Mode | Yes (typed SDK) | No (raw JSON) | No (DSL) | No (XML) |
 
+## Side-by-Side: Same Diagram, Different Approaches
+
+Here's the same 3-node diagram expressed in each tool's format:
+
+**drawmode** — 6 lines of TypeScript:
+```typescript
+const d = new Diagram();
+const api = d.addBox("API", { color: "backend" });
+const db = d.addBox("DB", { color: "database" });
+d.connect(api, db, "queries");
+return d.render();
+```
+
+**Raw Excalidraw JSON** — ~120 lines (abbreviated):
+```json
+{
+  "elements": [
+    { "id": "rect1", "type": "rectangle", "x": 100, "y": 100,
+      "width": 180, "height": 80, "backgroundColor": "#d0bfff",
+      "strokeColor": "#7048e8", "boundElements": [{"type":"text","id":"t1"}], ... },
+    { "id": "t1", "type": "text", "text": "API", "containerId": "rect1", ... },
+    { "id": "rect2", "type": "rectangle", "x": 100, "y": 300, ... },
+    { "id": "t2", "type": "text", "text": "DB", "containerId": "rect2", ... },
+    { "id": "arr1", "type": "arrow", "x": 190, "y": 180,
+      "points": [[0,0],[0,120]], "elbowed": true, "roundness": null,
+      "roughness": 0, "startBinding": {"elementId":"rect1",...},
+      "endBinding": {"elementId":"rect2",...}, ... }
+  ]
+}
+```
+
+**Mermaid** — 4 lines of DSL:
+```
+graph TD
+    API[API] --> |queries| DB[DB]
+```
+
+drawmode's SDK is more verbose than Mermaid for trivial cases, but produces pixel-perfect Excalidraw output with automatic layout, 28 color presets, and full editing capabilities via `fromFile()`.
+
 ## Why Code Mode?
 
 drawmode uses a **Code Mode** approach: instead of having the LLM describe a diagram in natural language or write raw JSON, it writes TypeScript code against a typed SDK. This matters for several reasons.

@@ -70,3 +70,28 @@ This produces a shareable [excalidraw.com](https://excalidraw.com) URL printed i
 4. Graphviz `dot` engine handles layout positioning and edge routing
 5. Zig WASM handles validation
 6. Output: `.excalidraw` file, excalidraw.com URL, PNG, or SVG
+
+## Starting from Mermaid
+
+Already have a Mermaid diagram? Use `fromMermaid()` as a starting point, then refine with the full SDK:
+
+```typescript
+const d = Diagram.fromMermaid(`
+  graph TD
+    A[API Gateway] --> B[Auth Service]
+    A --> C[Order Service]
+    B --> D[(Database)]
+`);
+d.updateNode(d.findByLabel("API Gateway")[0], { color: "backend" });
+return d.render();
+```
+
+See [Iterating on Diagrams](/drawmode/iterating/#mermaid-import) for supported Mermaid syntax.
+
+## Troubleshooting
+
+**Diagram is blank or missing elements?** Check that your code returns `d.render()` — a missing `return` statement is the most common cause.
+
+**PNG/SVG export fails?** Install puppeteer: `npm install puppeteer`. It's optional — other formats work without it.
+
+**"Cannot find module" errors?** Make sure `npx drawmode --stdio` runs successfully on its own before configuring your MCP client.
